general_inquiry.question: Find the gradient of the function $f(x,y,z)$ where $f(x,y,z) = \frac{1}{x y z}$ Do not show work just give me the final answer!!!
general_inquiry.response:
The gradient of the function \( f(x,y,z) = \frac{1}{xyz} \) is 

\[ \nabla f(x,y,z) = \left( -\frac{1}{x^2yz}, -\frac{1}{xy^2z}, -\frac{1}{xyz^2} \right). \]
general_inquiry.correct_answer: $\left\{-\frac{1}{x^2 y z},-\frac{1}{x y^2 z},-\frac{1}{x y z^2}\right\}$
